skill.name: content-research-writer
skill.description: Helps writers research, outline, draft, cite, and revise long-form content while preserving their voice and audience goals.
skill.summary: Research and draft structured articles with citations, feedback, and polish.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 35 Ruby or shell backtick executions and three weak cryptography blockers, but review found Markdown prose, command examples, and templates rather than executable code. The remaining publish risk is low: the skill may prompt local file creation, web research, and sharing drafts or style samples.

low_findings.title: Markdown command examples misclassified as executable code
low_findings.description: The static shell execution findings are false positives. The reviewed lines are fenced Markdown examples and a local setup suggestion using mkdir, cd, and touch, with no script wrapper or automatic execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backtick regions are Markdown fences, not Ruby backtick execution. I found no code file, script entry point, or instruction that automatically runs these examples.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography blocker findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits occur in natural-language description and workflow text. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or cryptographic library usage is present at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about writing quality, article format, and feedback workflow. They do not contain cryptographic operations or weak algorithm references in a security context.

low_findings.title: User drafts and writing samples may contain sensitive information
low_findings.description: The skill asks users to share style samples and draft content so the assistant can preserve voice. This is expected for writing work, but users should avoid confidential or regulated material unless their environment permits it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly asks for writing samples and drafts, which can include sensitive content. I found no evidence of exfiltration or hidden collection, so the concern remains low.

content.value_statement: Long-form content is hard to structure, research, and polish without losing your voice. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan, source, draft, review, and refine written work.
actual_capabilities: Builds article, newsletter, tutorial, and thought leadership outlines from a topic or thesis.
Identifies research gaps and suggests credible sources for claims, examples, and data.
Improves hooks, introductions, transitions, section flow, and conclusions.
Reviews individual sections with feedback on clarity, evidence, structure, and style.
Formats citations as inline references, numbered references, or footnotes.
Provides final draft reviews with pre-publish checks and polish suggestions.
limitations: Source quality depends on the research tools and sources available in the active environment.
It does not guarantee factual accuracy without user review of cited sources.
It cannot preserve voice well without examples or clear tone guidance.
It is designed for writing workflows, not automated publishing or content management.

use_cases.title: Plan a research-backed blog post
use_cases.description: Turn a topic into a clear outline, research list, draft plan, and citation approach.

use_cases.title: Improve a technical tutorial
use_cases.description: Review explanations, examples, steps, troubleshooting notes, and source references for clarity.

use_cases.title: Refine executive thought leadership
use_cases.description: Strengthen the thesis, evidence, hook, flow, and voice before publication.

prompt_templates.title: Create a starter outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new article idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an outline for an article about [topic]. The audience is [audience], and the goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Research and cite key claims
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the claims in this outline and suggest credible citations. Mark any weak claims that need better evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Review a completed section
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this section for clarity, flow, evidence, and voice. Give specific edits and explain the reason for each change.

prompt_templates.title: Run a final editorial pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the full draft for argument strength, structure, citation quality, readability, and publication readiness. Provide prioritized revisions.

output_examples.input: Create an outline for an article about AI in product management.
output_examples.output: A structured outline with a hook, introduction, three main sections, conclusion, and a research to-do list for missing evidence.

output_examples.input: Improve this introduction and make the hook stronger.
output_examples.output: Three hook options, a short explanation for each option, and notes on audience fit and emotional impact.

output_examples.input: Review my final draft before I publish it.
output_examples.output: A final review covering strengths, structure, evidence gaps, readability, citation checks, and prioritized polish suggestions.

best_practices: Provide the audience, goal, format, target length, and tone before asking for an outline.
Verify each source yourself before publishing facts, quotes, or statistics.
Ask for feedback one section at a time when working on long drafts.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated citations without checking the original source.
Do not ask for voice matching without sharing style examples or tone rules.
Do not include confidential drafts unless your workspace policy allows it.

faq.question: Can this skill write a full article from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It works best when you provide the topic, audience, goal, tone, and source requirements.

faq.question: Does it add citations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest and format citations, but you should verify every source before publication.

faq.question: Can it preserve my writing voice?
faq.answer: Yes. Share writing samples and tone preferences so it can match style more closely.

faq.question: Is it only for blog posts?
faq.answer: No. It also supports newsletters, tutorials, case studies, documentation, and thought leadership drafts.

faq.question: Does it need web access?
faq.answer: Research quality improves with web or source access. Without it, it can still outline, edit, and identify research gaps.

faq.question: Can it publish content automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on research, drafting, feedback, citations, and final review.
